# guardicore_user_group (Data Source)

Reads a user group from Akamai Guardicore Segmentation.

Use this data source to reference existing user groups, including system-managed groups that cannot be modified by Terraform. The `system_managed` attribute indicates whether the group is managed by the platform.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up a user group by ID.
data "guardicore_user_group" "by_id" {
  id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000040"
}

# Look up a user group by title.
data "guardicore_user_group" "platform_engineers" {
  title = "Platform Engineers"
}

output "user_group_details" {
  value = {
    id                    = data.guardicore_user_group.platform_engineers.id
    title                 = data.guardicore_user_group.platform_engineers.title
    orchestrations_groups = data.guardicore_user_group.platform_engineers.orchestrations_groups
  }
}

# Look up a system-managed user group (e.g., a local system group).
# System-managed groups are created by the platform and cannot be modified
# by Terraform.
data "guardicore_user_group" "local_admins" {
  title = "Local Administrators"
}

output "user_group_system_managed" {
  value = data.guardicore_user_group.local_admins.system_managed
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the user group. Either id or title must be specified.
- `title` (String) The title of the user group. Used to look up a user group by title.

### Read-Only

- `managed_by` (String) Identifies who manages this user group. `terraform` for user-managed groups, or `system` for platform-managed groups.
- `orchestrations_groups` (Attributes List) List of orchestration groups included in the user group. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--orchestrations_groups))
- `system_managed` (Boolean) Whether this user group is system-managed. System-managed groups cannot be updated or deleted by Terraform. Use the `guardicore_user_group` data source to reference them.

<a id="nestedatt--orchestrations_groups"></a>
### Nested Schema for `orchestrations_groups`

Read-Only:

- `groups` (List of String) List of group IDs within the orchestration.
- `orchestration_id` (String) The orchestration ID.
